sections.paragraph:
to tear away, Jerem. 22:24 . Applied figuratively in a military sense, to draw away, to cut off any one (from a place), Jud. 20:32 . See Niphal and Hiphil. Part. pass. נָתוּק castrated, Lev 22:24.

Piel, to tear up or off, as roots, Eze 17:9 to burst bonds, Psal. 2:3 107:14 a yoke, Isa 58:6 to wound by tearing, Eze 23:34.

Hiphil, metaph. to separate out, Jer 12:3 to draw away from, to cut off from any place, Jos 8:6.

Niphal-

(1) to be torn away, to be broken, e.g. used of a thread, a cord, Isa 5:27 Jer 10:20 Jud. 16:9 Ecc 4:12 a rope, Isa 33:20. Metaph. Job 17:11, “the counsels of my mind are broken off;” i.e. frustrated.

(2) to be torn out, as from a tent, Job 18:14. Pregnant construction, Jos 4:18, “and when the soles of the feet of the priests were plucked up (from the muddy channel and set) on the dry land.”

(3) to be separated, Jer 6:29 followed by מִן to be drawn away from, Jos 8:16.

Hophal הָנְתַּק i.q. Niphal No. 3, Jdg 20:31. Hence-
